code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a university (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent sentence. The function builds a phrase for each triple and joins them
    with commas, adding “and” before the last clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple verb mapping for common predicates
    verb_map = {
        "city": "is located in",
        "country": "is in",
        "state": "is in",
        "location": "is located in",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "river": "is traversed by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at an elevation of",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed",
        "latinName": "has latin name",
        "motto": "has the motto",
        "anthem": "has anthem",
        "religion": "follows",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "leaderTitle": "has a leader titled",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
        "legislature": "has legislature",
        "sportGoverningBody": "is governed by",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
        "established": "was established in",
        "rector": "has rector",
        "dean": "has dean",
        "director": "has director",
        "president": "has president",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "academicStaffSize": "has an academic staff of",
        "numberOfStudents": "has",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official colour(s)",
        "outlookRanking": "holds an Outlook ranking of",
        "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given technical campus status by",
        "campus": "has campus at",
        "staff": "has staff of",
    }

    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        verb = verb_map.get(triple.predicate, "has")
        # For numeric predicates, adjust wording
        if triple.predicate in {
            "academicStaffSize", "numberOfStudents", "numberOfPostgraduateStudents",
            "numberOfUndergraduateStudents", "numberOfDoctoralStudents", "staff"
        }:
            return f"{triple.subject} {verb} {triple.object}"
        # For ranking or similar numeric values
        if triple.predicate == "outlookRanking":
            return f"{triple.subject} {verb} {triple.object}"
        # Generic handling
        return f"{triple.subject} {verb} {triple.object}"

    # Build clauses
    clauses = [phrase(t) for t in triples]

    # Join clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
